DECLARATION OF NEWTON OLDFATHER FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. CCSF HAS NOT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN. FIRST, CCSF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT IDENTIFY THE SPECIFIC TOILET AT ISSUE. IN ANY EVENT, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO. (UMF NO. 2.) THE EVIDENCE PROFFERED DOES NOT SUPPORT UMF NO. 2. SECOND, DEFENDANT CCSF FAILED TO ADDRESS ALL OF THE FACTS PLED IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT CCSF FOCUSES EXCLUSIVELY ON THE NOTICE PRONG OF THE STATUTE, AND HAS NOT ADDRESSED THE ALLEGATION THAT THE CONDITION WAS CREATED BY CCSF EMPLOYEES PURSUANT TO GOV. CODE 835(A)-IN WHICH CASE NOTICE WOULD NOT BE NECESSARY. (FIRST. AM. COMPLAINT, P. 4, PREM.L-4B.) THEREFORE, EVEN IF CCSF HAD SHOWN THAT PLAINTIFF COULD NOT PROVE NOTICE (WHICH THE COURT DOES NOT DECIDE), CCSF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PREVAIL ON HIS CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR # 9581

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-28-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NIKOLAUS W. REED, TEL. NO. NWR LAW, TEL. NO. 940-7766 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NEWTON OLDFATHER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4283 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. CCSF has not met its initial burden. First, CCSF has not shown that Plaintiff cannot identify the specific toilet at issue. In any event, there is a triable issue of fact whether Plaintiff can do so. (UMF no. 2.) The evidence proffered does not support UMF no. 2. Second, Defendant CCSF failed to address all of the facts pled in the First Amended Complaint. Defendant CCSF focuses exclusively on the notice prong of the statute, and has not addressed the allegation that the condition was created by CCSF employees pursuant to Gov. Code 835(a)-in which case notice would not be necessary. (First. Am. Complaint, p. 4, Prem.L-4b.) Therefore, even if CCSF had shown that Plaintiff could not prove notice (which the Court does not decide), CCSF has failed to show that Plaintiff cannot prevail on his claim. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR # 9581 =302/PJB
